positive.literal: solo, full body, standing, single character, feet on ground
full figure visible from head to feet

negative.literal: multiple views, multiple heads, extra heads, character sheet, reference sheet, floating, cropped feet, disembodied
cropped, out of frame, feet cut off, close-up, portrait

laneRules: fullbody is ONE figure, head-to-feet, feet planted on the ground — never a turnaround or reference sheet (the 14/14 toss failed on duplicate heads + floating figures).
The bust and the sprite share one identity (both are FACE kinds and check out together); the tailor keeps the face consistent via identityRef.
Slots are plain words only; no weight/embedding syntax reaches the model — structure lives in this template.
OWNER FIX (v2) — WHOLE FIGURE IN FRAME: 'full figure visible from head to feet' asserted; 'cropped, out of frame, feet cut off, close-up, portrait' negated so the sprite is never a bust in disguise.

ERA LAW (all entity lanes) — a clothing/gear slot MUST carry an era qualifier (e.g. 'medieval fantasy clothing'); without it the model defaults to modern dress (a 'wool robe' rendered as a bathrobe). mapNpcToSlots injects the world's era descriptor (world.era) into the attire slot when the world data carries one; a world with no era field is a WORLD-DATA GAP (reported, never invented here).
